destinations.evidence: The nodebuilder creates the configured block verifier with the node provider and EVM config and passes None for executor_registry_contract, leaving the verifier to resolve its default registry source.

equivalence_argument: In the split v2.2 architecture, nodebuilder owns Ritual node executor assembly; its pool-aware builder wires the verifier and intentionally provides no registry override.
notes: Mapped in the nodebuilder dependency because the port workspace delegates node construction there.
rationale: The primary commit updates new_with_async_validation to include the executor_registry_contract argument and deliberately passes None with a comment that the default should come from env/genesis, which the commit message says is required for contract-based SPC verification. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: When the Ritual node builder creates a BlockVerifier with async commitment validation, it must pass no executor_registry_contract override so the verifier resolves the executor registry contract from its default environment or genesis source.
suggested_falsification_tests: Instantiate the node executor builder with async validation enabled and a BlockVerifier test double or observable configuration; assert the executor_registry_contract argument is None and that the verifier subsequently resolves the configured default registry address.
summary: Nodebuilder constructs the block verifier without an executor-registry override.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Instantiate build_evm_with_pool and assert the created BlockVerifier receives None for executor_registry_contract and is installed in RitualBlockExecutorProvider.
title: Block verifier uses default executor registry
